stem: A rectangular prism has length $5 \text{ cm}$, width $3 \text{ cm}$ and height $4 \text{ cm}$. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
hint: Use $V = lwh$. Multiply the three dimensions together.

stem: A rectangular prism has length $8 \text{ cm}$, width $2 \text{ cm}$ and height $3 \text{ cm}$. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
hint: Use $V = lwh$ and multiply all three dimensions.

stem: A rectangular prism has length $6 \text{ cm}$, width $4 \text{ cm}$ and height $5 \text{ cm}$. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
hint: The volume of a rectangular prism is $V = lwh$.

stem: A rectangular prism has length $7 \text{ cm}$, width $4 \text{ cm}$ and height $2 \text{ cm}$. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with base $6 \text{ cm}$ and perpendicular height $4 \text{ cm}$. The prism is $10 \text{ cm}$ long. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
hint: Find the area of the triangular cross-section first: $A = \tfrac{1}{2}bh$. Then multiply by the length.
explanation: Area of cross-section: $A = \tfrac{1}{2} \times 6 \times 4 = 12 \text{ cm}^2$. Volume: $V = 12 \times 10 = 120 \text{ cm}^3$

stem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with base $8 \text{ cm}$ and perpendicular height $3 \text{ cm}$. The prism is $7 \text{ cm}$ long. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
hint: Use $V = \tfrac{1}{2}bh \times l$ where $b$ and $h$ are the base and height of the triangle, and $l$ is the prism length.
explanation: Area of cross-section: $A = \tfrac{1}{2} \times 8 \times 3 = 12 \text{ cm}^2$. Volume: $V = 12 \times 7 = 84 \text{ cm}^3$

stem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with base $5 \text{ cm}$ and perpendicular height $6 \text{ cm}$. The prism is $9 \text{ cm}$ long. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
hint: Calculate the triangular area first: $A = \tfrac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 6$. Then multiply by the prism length.
explanation: Area of cross-section: $A = \tfrac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 6 = 15 \text{ cm}^2$. Volume: $V = 15 \times 9 = 135 \text{ cm}^3$

stem: A triangular prism has a right-angled triangular cross-section with base $10 \text{ cm}$ and perpendicular height $4 \text{ cm}$. The prism is $8 \text{ cm}$ long. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
hint: The cross-sectional area is $\tfrac{1}{2} \times 10 \times 4$. Multiply this by the prism length.
explanation: Area of cross-section: $A = \tfrac{1}{2} \times 10 \times 4 = 20 \text{ cm}^2$. Volume: $V = 20 \times 8 = 160 \text{ cm}^3$

stem: A prism has an L-shaped cross-section formed by joining a $6 \text{ cm} \times 4 \text{ cm}$ rectangle and a $3 \text{ cm} \times 2 \text{ cm}$ rectangle. The prism is $8 \text{ cm}$ long. Find its volume in $\text{cm}^3$.
hint: Find the area of the L-shaped cross-section by adding the areas of the two rectangles. Then multiply by the prism length.
explanation: Area of L-shape: $6 \times 4 + 3 \times 2 = 24 + 6 = 30 \text{ cm}^2$. Volume: $V = 30 \times 8 = 240 \text{ cm}^3$

stem: A rectangular water tank is $60 \text{ cm}$ long, $25 \text{ cm}$ wide and $30 \text{ cm}$ deep. Water fills $\tfrac{3}{4}$ of its capacity. How many litres of water are in the tank? ($1 \text{ litre} = 1000 \text{ cm}^3$)
hint: Find the full volume using $V = lwh$, take $\tfrac{3}{4}$ of it, then convert from $\text{cm}^3$ to litres by dividing by $1000$.
explanation: Full volume: $V = 60 \times 25 \times 30 = 45\,000 \text{ cm}^3$. Water: $\tfrac{3}{4} \times 45\,000 = 33\,750 \text{ cm}^3$. In litres: $33\,750 \div 1000 = 33.75 \text{ L}$

stem: A building has a cross-section made up of a rectangle $5 \text{ m}$ wide and $3 \text{ m}$ tall, topped by a triangle $5 \text{ m}$ wide and $2 \text{ m}$ tall. The building is $8 \text{ m}$ long. Find the total volume in $\text{m}^3$.
hint: Split the cross-section into a rectangle and a triangle. Find each area, add them, then multiply by the length.
explanation: Rectangular area: $5 \times 3 = 15 \text{ m}^2$. Triangular area: $\tfrac{1}{2} \times 5 \times 2 = 5 \text{ m}^2$. Total cross-sectional area: $15 + 5 = 20 \text{ m}^2$. Volume: $V = 20 \times 8 = 160 \text{ m}^3$

stem: A concrete ramp has a trapezoidal cross-section with parallel sides of $0.5 \text{ m}$ and $2 \text{ m}$, and a perpendicular width of $3 \text{ m}$. The ramp is $5 \text{ m}$ long. Find the volume of concrete in $\text{m}^3$.
hint: The area of a trapezium is $A = \tfrac{1}{2}(a + b)h$ where $a$ and $b$ are the parallel sides. Then multiply by the prism length.
explanation: Area of trapezium: $A = \tfrac{1}{2}(0.5 + 2) \times 3 = \tfrac{1}{2} \times 2.5 \times 3 = 3.75 \text{ m}^2$. Volume: $V = 3.75 \times 5 = 18.75 \text{ m}^3$
